title: Chapter Sixty-Seven: The Grand Teacher's Freedom
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The sect members bowed.

Not because they understood what he had done. Because they felt it -- the momentum of the Demon Roaring Blood Technique, stripped of its madness, radiating from him like heat from a forge without the smoke. Superior blood technique energy that made the Blood Summoning Technique in their own veins tremble and submit.

"Such pure energy waves," someone whispered.

"Could he be pure blood of the Sect Leader's lineage?"

"Grand Teacher! Grand Teacher!"

"Lead our sect to Beijing!"

Fanaticism. Desperation dressed in worship. They wanted black hair. They wanted to be free of the mark that branded them as monsters to the outside world.

Gao Zong-xue stood on his platform, mouth open, the rally he had orchestrated hijacked by the very person he had tried to embarrass. The man's jaw worked, but no words came.

Yeon-shin turned and walked away without responding. No regular member dared follow.

Only the Seventh Apostle kept pace, her single red eye bright with excitement.

"I did not know you would achieve this overnight. Your vessel of martial arts is broader than I imagined."

He glanced at her, then forward.

"I need to look around the main hall. Not just once. Periodically."

"Why?"

"To extract new verses, inspiration will not come from sitting in a room. I need wind and nature. I need to observe your sect members' martial qualities -- their breathing, their steps, their gestures, even the thoughts that form the basis of their mental focus. To circulate internal energy through optimal paths, I must know everything about the practitioner."

He stopped walking. Looked at her directly.

"It is the same for the Blood Summoning Technique. I will need to experience the Blood Flame Sect's spirit to modify it into a versatile martial art."

She was panting. He had only offered her the excuse of tailored martial arts, and she trembled like a child promised a gift.

*She sees me as prey already caught.*

It worked. He gained freedom of movement -- anywhere in the compound, with the Seventh Apostle as escort.

"Starting with the Sect Leader's hall courtyard."

---

He moved through the headquarters methodically. The thirty-six marble steps leading to the Sect Leader's hall. The railings carved from Dali Kingdom stone. The walls, the rafters, the courtyard paving.

At each point, he paused. Touched a surface. Raised his true qi.

The technique was layered deception. First, the Demon Roaring Blood Technique -- its energy waves visible and expected. Beneath that shell, hidden like a blade within a walking stick: the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm.

He drew on the principles of the Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation to lock the embedded power into the stone. The energy sank deep and went still. No detectable waves. No trace.

*Unless activated, formation mediums are just still objects. Even the Sect Leader cannot sense them.*

"It is sturdy," he said, stroking a marble wall with the detached air of an artist studying his canvas.

"You have discerning eyes," the Seventh Apostle said brightly. "All structures in our sect contain decades of history. I will show you noble scenery that can only be seen here."

"All of it is inspiration. Impressive."

Day after day. Sunset and dawn crossed more than thirty times. A month of quiet, patient work.

Railings. Stairs. Rafters. Dirt ground. Roofs. He planted the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm throughout the Blood Flame Sect, concentrating around the Sect Leader's hall. Each touch was a deception. Each pause to admire a carved pillar or run his fingers along a stone balustrade was a weapon planted. A hidden formation within their formation.

And each day, he watched them feed.

The Blood Flame Sect's true nature revealed itself in the small hours. Commoners dragged in from surrounding villages. Martial artists captured on the roads. The sect members did not kill quickly. They drained -- pressing their palms to the victims' backs, drawing true qi and blood essence through the Blood Summoning Technique's star absorption. The bodies they left behind were husks, wrung dry like cloth in a washwoman's hands. The sect's medical division burned them in pits to prevent plague.

He said nothing. Did nothing.

*Not yet.*

The word became a mantra. He repeated it when the screaming started. Repeated it when the burning pits belched smoke into the dawn sky. Repeated it when the Seventh Apostle returned from her nightly hunts with blood on her lips and a smile meant only for him.

*Not yet. Not yet. Not yet.*

He had to survive this place to destroy it. And so he walked its corridors like a loyal dog, touched its walls like a reverent student, and loaded each one with the power that would tear them down.

---

The Seventh Apostle came to him with killing intent leaking from every joint.

"Desolate Sect's forces are searching the surroundings," she said. "Shaolin, Zhongnan, Mount Hua. Branch sects have gathered. Easily over five hundred, with white and blue ranks among them."

Yeon-shin sat cross-legged. "It was obvious. Bringing me here was a risk. They have swept all areas within a month's distance."

"I will not give you to anyone." Her white hand reached out, long fingers stroking his hair. The power in each joint was tremendous -- a reminder, wrapped in the gesture of tenderness. "Do not harbor fleeting hopes. You are already mine."

"You said no one has found this place for hundreds of years."

"The Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation is not human realm learning. Even a million-strong army could pass and perceive nothing."

He stared at her. "Yet you are going on an expedition."

"Are you worried about me? My possession! I am so happy!"

*Woong!*

He raised the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm through his meridians. A reflex. She pulled back, still smiling.

"When will you soften toward me?"

He stood and turned away.

These people's staple food was not grain but people. Every day they captured martial artists and commoners, drained their blood and qi, and burned the husks. Even their medical arts existed only to prevent plague from the rot.

*Time to load today's power into the Sect Leader's hall.*

He walked through the compound toward the formation's boundary. The Seventh Apostle had gone on her expedition. Gao Zong-xue trailed him at a distance -- the Third Apostle's son had been shadowing him for days, probing, prodding.

The man stepped into his path. "At this point, I have a thought. I heard the Grand Teacher has talent worthy of discussing the world's finest." A sneer played at the edges of his courtesy. "Though I have paid respects until now, as a sect member I am concerned about the lack of proper merit."

"Say what you want to say."

The reply came as sound transmission, private and venomous.

*-Kill yourself. You broken string lowborn.*

Yeon-shin felt a breeze brush his collar. A whisper against his ear -- familiar. Warm. He had felt it before, during his first mission. Cheong Myeong's demonstration of body technique, approaching like wind carrying elf power.

*The Sect Lord.*

She was near.

"Think as you wish," he said, and turned away.

He reached the outskirts. The boundary of the Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation rippled before him -- space bending, the air trembling with energies that did not belong to the human realm.

And beyond it, she stood.

"Ah."

He swallowed. His throat had gone tight.

Light green martial clothes like palace attire. Head slightly raised. Milky white skin emitting a smooth radiance that made all other scenery lose its color. Sunlight formed a halo around her slender nose bridge.

Her long pale green hair flowed past her shoulders and down her back, and the vegetation around her had settled into stillness, as if every blade of grass recognized its master.

*Not human presence. Not at all.*

She turned her head.

Green eyes. Brilliant. Containing the sun and an overwhelming abyss behind it.

His lifeline. His salvation.

Their gazes brushed past each other for an instant, then seemed not to meet. She stood still. Sunlight fractured along the line of her jaw.

*Surely she can perceive me. Surely the formation cannot fool her.*

His eyes wavered. For the first time since entering this place, he could not hold steady.

*-You could take more. I will give you one day.*

A whisper like wind through leaves. Then she took one step, and leaves scattered like flower petals, and she was gone. As if asking when she had come.

He stood alone at the boundary.

*I will give you one day.*

The words settled warm in his chest -- the first warmth he had felt since the night they took him.

He could escape.

*What more can I take?*

The question followed him back through the compound, through the fading light, through the long hours until dawn.

---
